name: CoWoS advanced packaging (TSMC)
description: TSMC's Chip-on-Wafer-on-Substrate 2.5D packaging is the single point of integration for HBM + GPU/ASIC dies. Every NVIDIA, AMD, and most hyperscaler-custom AI accelerators must pass through CoWoS. CoWoS-L (with RDL interposer) is the volume node for Rubin and MI400-class parts.
stackPosition: GPU/ASIC die (TSMC N3/N3P) + HBM stacks → CoWoS interposer (silicon interposer or RDL on glass) → C4 bump attach → underfill → substrate (ABF) → BGA → module → server. CoWoS is the only mass-volume 2.5D process that NVIDIA and AMD have qualified; TSMC's expansion in AP6 and AP7 is the binding constraint for total AI accelerator wafer-equivalent supply.

supplyStructure.name: Amkor
supplyStructure.note: US-AZ pilot for Apple/AMD; not at NVIDIA volume yet

companies.name: TSMC
companies.note: Owns ~95% of CoWoS; pricing power on advanced packaging surcharge

companies.name: Amkor Technology
companies.note: Second-source CoWoS bet; AZ fab tied to Apple/AMD onshoring

companies.name: ASE Technology
companies.note: FOCoS-Bridge; alternative architecture for cost-down packaging

companies.name: Applied Materials
companies.note: Hybrid-bonding and TSV tools for advanced packaging lines

companies.name: BE Semiconductor
companies.note: Hybrid bonding tools — overlap with HBM4 thesis

insight: CoWoS is the single most important constraint in AI compute, but the constraint is shifting from raw wafer capacity to reticle-stitching geometry. As Rubin and MI400 push past 3-reticle designs, *interposer area per accelerator* matters more than wafer count. The narrow-window trade is hybrid-bonding suppliers (BESI, applied) that enable the next geometry doubling. The widow-maker trade is CoWoS-S enablers — that node is being deprecated faster than consensus expects in favor of CoWoS-L on glass.

debates.question: Does Amkor's AZ CoWoS line reach NVIDIA-qualified volume by end of 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Apple and AMD likely first; NVIDIA qualification slips to 2028

debates.question: Does CoWoS oversupply emerge in 2H 2027 as new capacity outruns demand?
debates.currentBelief: Tight through 2027; modest oversupply possible mid-2028 if accelerator unit growth normalizes
